answers:
**1. What happens for an existing clinic that has never opened this setting?**
Reminders will be sent automatically. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:
```python
def auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):
    if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
        return True
    return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders"))
```
Line 11-12: if the key is missing (as it is for `tenant-b` in `seeds/tenants.json:3`, `"settings": {}`), the function returns `True`. The batch job at `slotwise/backend/app/jobs/reminders.py:25` (`if auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):`) then sends SMS and email reminders for that clinic's upcoming appointments (lines 26-30) — with no other gate in the code path.

**2. Is that default the right behavior?**
No, this default is backwards for this feature and should be inverted. Per the skill's blast-radius rule: automated outbound messaging to patients is exactly the category that must default to **off** when unset, not on. Concretely, if this ships as-is, every existing clinic that has never seen this new setting — the overwhelming majority, since it just shipped in SLOT-214 — will start automatically texting/emailing patients without ever opting in. That is a real product/compliance risk (unsolicited patient contact, possible SMS consent/TCPA-adjacent issues depending on jurisdiction), not a convenience nicety like the calendar view toggle it was copied from. I would change line 11-12 in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py` so that a missing/`None` value returns `False` instead of `True`:
```python
def auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):
    if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
        return False
    return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders"))
```
I'd also flag a secondary inconsistency this reveals: the settings UI (`slotwise/frontend/src/pages/ClinicSettings.tsx:7`, `checked={!!settings.auto_send_reminders}`) already renders the checkbox as **unchecked** when the setting is unset — so admins looking at Clinic B's settings today see the toggle "off," while the backend is actually treating it as "on" and would silently send reminders. The UI's unchecked-by-default rendering is actually the correct signal here; it's the backend resolver that's out of step with it.

**3. How did this null-handling get decided, and was that a sound basis?**
It wasn't decided at all for this flag — it was copy-pasted. `git log` on `feature_flags.py` shows only two commits: `25509a7` ("SLOT-190 tenant settings and calendar view preference") introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled` with the "default True on missing" pattern, which is a reasonable choice there (a UI display preference, low blast radius, and defaulting existing tenants "on" to the new default view avoids disrupting them). `aa4cf8d` ("Add automatic appointment reminders (SLOT-214)") then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` directly below it with the identical `if ... is None: return True` structure (diff shows it added verbatim as the second function in the same file). Nothing in the SLOT-214 diff (which also touched `jobs/reminders.py` and `ClinicSettings.tsx`) shows any distinct reasoning applied to the new flag's null case — it's the same three lines with a different key name. That is not a sound basis: this is precisely the anti-pattern the skill calls out ("Never copy-paste one resolver's null-handling into a new flag in the other category without re-deciding this explicitly") — a convenience-flag default was reused for an outbound-automated-action flag without re-evaluating blast radius.

**4. What to verify/change before enabling this feature?**
- Invert the missing/null default in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` to `False` (see #2), so existing clinics must explicitly opt in.
- Add an env-level master switch (e.g. `REMINDERS_ENABLED`) ANDed with the per-tenant value, per the skill's recipe step 3, so the feature can be globally killed/rolled back without touching every tenant's settings, and so it can be staged (enabled only in a subset of environments) before general availability.
- Decide and implement real messaging: `MessagingClient` (`slotwise/backend/app/messaging_client.py`) is currently just an in-memory stub with no real SMS/email transport — confirm what production client will replace it and that it fails safely (doesn't silently no-op or silently succeed) before this is wired to real patient contact info.
- Reconcile the UI/backend mismatch: decide whether the UI checkbox and the backend resolver should both reflect "off by default," and add a test asserting `auto_send_reminders_enabled` returns `False` for a tenant with `settings == {}}` or missing key, to lock in the corrected default.
- Write down a rollback affordance (skill step 6) — confirm ops can flip the env-level master off quickly if reminders misfire (wrong recipients, duplicate sends) after enabling.
- Consider malformed values too: `bool(tenant.settings.get(...))` will treat any truthy JSON value (e.g. a non-empty string like `"false"`) as enabled; confirm the settings-write path validates this is a real boolean before it reaches this resolver.
report:
## Plan
I set out to verify the current behavior of the per-clinic `auto_send_reminders` toggle in Slotwise before the team enables the reminder-sending feature: what happens when a clinic has never touched the setting, whether that default is appropriate given the feature's blast radius (automated outbound SMS/email), how the current handling came to be (git history), and what should be checked before flipping this on. I used the `config-and-secrets-hygiene` skill's guidance on per-tenant flag null/missing semantics ("blast radius" rule) and its recipe for adding new flags as my primary framework.

## Actions
1. Read `.claude/skills/config-and-secrets-hygiene/SKILL.md` in full — this shaped my review, specifically the section "Per-tenant flags: the null/missing semantics is a product decision, not a coin flip," which says convenience-only flags should default on for existing tenants, but anything that "can trigger outbound messaging, spend money, or take an automated action on a user's behalf" must invert the default so missing/null/malformed means **off**.
2. `Glob **/feature_flags.py` and `**/reminders.py` — located the files at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py` and `slotwise/backend/app/jobs/reminders.py` (repo root is `slotwise/`, not the outer directory).
3. Read `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py` — found `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)` (lines 10–13), which returns `True` when `tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders")` is `None`.
4. Read `slotwise/backend/app/jobs/reminders.py` — confirmed the batch job calls `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)` (line 25) as the sole gate before sending SMS/email reminders to every upcoming appointment for that tenant; no env-level master switch is checked anywhere in this file.
5. Ran `git log --oneline --all -- backend/app/feature_flags.py` (inside `slotwise/`) — found two commits: `25509a7` (SLOT-190, added `compact_calendar_view_enabled`) and `aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214, added `auto_send_reminders_enabled`).
6. Ran `git show aa4cf8d --stat`, `git show aa4cf8d -- backend/app/feature_flags.py`, `git show 25509a7 -- backend/app/feature_flags.py`, and `git log -p aa4cf8d -1` — confirmed SLOT-214's diff adds `auto_send_reminders_enabled` with the exact same `if ... is None: return True` shape as the pre-existing `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, and that SLOT-214 also touched `frontend/src/pages/ClinicSettings.tsx`.
7. Read `slotwise/frontend/src/pages/ClinicSettings.tsx` — the settings checkbox renders `checked={!!settings.auto_send_reminders}`, which evaluates to unchecked/false whenever the setting is unset.
8. Read `slotwise/backend/app/messaging_client.py` — confirmed it's a stub client (records sends in memory) with no real transport, but its `send_sms`/`send_email` interface is what real messaging would plug into.
9. Read `slotwise/seeds/tenants.json` — confirmed Clinic B (`tenant-b`) has `"settings": {}`, i.e., no `auto_send_reminders` key at all, representing exactly the "never opened this setting" case.

## Answers

**1. What happens for an existing clinic that has never opened this setting?**
Reminders will be sent automatically. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:
```python
def auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):
    if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
        return True
    return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders"))
```
Line 11-12: if the key is missing (as it is for `tenant-b` in `seeds/tenants.json:3`, `"settings": {}`), the function returns `True`. The batch job at `slotwise/backend/app/jobs/reminders.py:25` (`if auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):`) then sends SMS and email reminders for that clinic's upcoming appointments (lines 26-30) — with no other gate in the code path.

**2. Is that default the right behavior?**
No, this default is backwards for this feature and should be inverted. Per the skill's blast-radius rule: automated outbound messaging to patients is exactly the category that must default to **off** when unset, not on. Concretely, if this ships as-is, every existing clinic that has never seen this new setting — the overwhelming majority, since it just shipped in SLOT-214 — will start automatically texting/emailing patients without ever opting in. That is a real product/compliance risk (unsolicited patient contact, possible SMS consent/TCPA-adjacent issues depending on jurisdiction), not a convenience nicety like the calendar view toggle it was copied from. I would change line 11-12 in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py` so that a missing/`None` value returns `False` instead of `True`:
```python
def auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant):
    if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
        return False
    return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders"))
```
I'd also flag a secondary inconsistency this reveals: the settings UI (`slotwise/frontend/src/pages/ClinicSettings.tsx:7`, `checked={!!settings.auto_send_reminders}`) already renders the checkbox as **unchecked** when the setting is unset — so admins looking at Clinic B's settings today see the toggle "off," while the backend is actually treating it as "on" and would silently send reminders. The UI's unchecked-by-default rendering is actually the correct signal here; it's the backend resolver that's out of step with it.

**3. How did this null-handling get decided, and was that a sound basis?**
It wasn't decided at all for this flag — it was copy-pasted. `git log` on `feature_flags.py` shows only two commits: `25509a7` ("SLOT-190 tenant settings and calendar view preference") introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled` with the "default True on missing" pattern, which is a reasonable choice there (a UI display preference, low blast radius, and defaulting existing tenants "on" to the new default view avoids disrupting them). `aa4cf8d` ("Add automatic appointment reminders (SLOT-214)") then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` directly below it with the identical `if ... is None: return True` structure (diff shows it added verbatim as the second function in the same file). Nothing in the SLOT-214 diff (which also touched `jobs/reminders.py` and `ClinicSettings.tsx`) shows any distinct reasoning applied to the new flag's null case — it's the same three lines with a different key name. That is not a sound basis: this is precisely the anti-pattern the skill calls out ("Never copy-paste one resolver's null-handling into a new flag in the other category without re-deciding this explicitly") — a convenience-flag default was reused for an outbound-automated-action flag without re-evaluating blast radius.

**4. What to verify/change before enabling this feature?**
- Invert the missing/null default in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` to `False` (see #2), so existing clinics must explicitly opt in.
- Add an env-level master switch (e.g. `REMINDERS_ENABLED`) ANDed with the per-tenant value, per the skill's recipe step 3, so the feature can be globally killed/rolled back without touching every tenant's settings, and so it can be staged (enabled only in a subset of environments) before general availability.
- Decide and implement real messaging: `MessagingClient` (`slotwise/backend/app/messaging_client.py`) is currently just an in-memory stub with no real SMS/email transport — confirm what production client will replace it and that it fails safely (doesn't silently no-op or silently succeed) before this is wired to real patient contact info.
- Reconcile the UI/backend mismatch: decide whether the UI checkbox and the backend resolver should both reflect "off by default," and add a test asserting `auto_send_reminders_enabled` returns `False` for a tenant with `settings == {}}` or missing key, to lock in the corrected default.
- Write down a rollback affordance (skill step 6) — confirm ops can flip the env-level master off quickly if reminders misfire (wrong recipients, duplicate sends) after enabling.
- Consider malformed values too: `bool(tenant.settings.get(...))` will treat any truthy JSON value (e.g. a non-empty string like `"false"`) as enabled; confirm the settings-write path validates this is a real boolean before it reaches this resolver.

## Next steps
If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (a) flip the default in `feature_flags.py:11-12` to `False`; (b) add an env-level master flag check in `jobs/reminders.py` ANDed with the per-tenant check; (c) add a unit test for the missing-key case in both `compact_calendar_view_enabled` (documenting the intentional on-default) and `auto_send_reminders_enabled` (documenting the intentional off-default) so the two are not accidentally re-synced in a future refactor; (d) confirm with the team what real messaging provider replaces `MessagingClient` and whether it needs its own credential/config gating (per this same skill's env-var guidance) before go-live.

## Confidence and gaps
High confidence on items 1–3: these are directly observed in code and git history I read in full, not inferred. Item 2's recommendation (invert to off) is a judgment call, but it's the one the project's own hygiene skill prescribes for this exact blast-radius category, so I'm confident it's the right call to bring to the team rather than a purely personal opinion. Gaps: I did not find any admin-facing audit log, deploy config, or env-var reference documenting this flag (per skill recipe steps 4–5) — I don't know if one exists outside this repo (e.g., in an ops wiki), so I can't confirm whether documentation/rollback affordances already exist elsewhere. I also did not find the actual production messaging integration (only the stub `MessagingClient`), so I can't verify real-world delivery behavior, retry/error handling, or opt-out/consent handling beyond what's in this repo.
